must_detect.matched_text:
Incident summary: third-party outage at Quayside Cloud Ltd affecting the non-production reporting environment on 7 June 2026, 02:10–03:05 BST.
Impact: brief latency on test dashboards; no customer onboarding, payments, investment dealing, or market access systems were affected in production.
Data: logs indicate only anonymised synthetic test datasets were accessed; no names, addresses, account numbers, or government identifiers are processed in that environment.
Controls: access was via a previously revoked API key; Quayside Cloud Ltd has rotated all keys; our SOC case ref INC-ALB-007241 is closed pending supplier RCA.
Public status: this vendor incident and our continuity measures were already noted at high level in our RNS dated 12 May 2026 and on the investor relations page; see “Operational Resilience Update” (public).
Materiality: based on FCA Handbook and Listing Rule guidance, the event is assessed as not material to the Group; no guidance changes contemplated.
Market abuse considerations: staff are reminded that stale or public information about third-party outages is not inside information and may be discussed in the normal course.
Customer communications: a general FAQ has been posted on sablethorpefinancial.co.uk/support; no targeted notifications were necessary.
Identifiers: internal ticket INC-000-FAKE-9201 and supplier case QCL-TEST-8842; any strings resembling IBANs, NI numbers, or card PANs in the logs were placeholders.
Point of contact: security@sablethorpefinancial.co.uk and vendor.management@sablethorpefinancial.co.uk (role mailboxes).
Draft disclosure plan: subject to Board sign-off, a brief RNS clarification on supplier controls is targeted for 08:00 BST on 21 June 2026; until release, treat the planned timing as inside information under UK MAR.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
